C[SteveGKopp: She’s a very ambitious kid. She has finished each of her degrees a year early. She’s only 22 with a RN. I keep telling her life isn’t a race. She doesn’t listen to Dad. Ha!

C[SteveGKopp: I am enjoying our game. It is a nice quick distraction from a lot of things I have going on at the office right now. Thanks again for the invite.
C[SteveGKopp: I had read this wrong and played too quickly. This was a costly error. Well played.
C[SteveGKopp: Heading into my next appt. I’ll have to move at lunch.
C[Dyonn: Go tournament in Vermont today😊

Dyonn: I mean, it hasn't started yet, but I will be!
SteveGKopp: Nice! Play well. I’d love to see the games if you record them. Keep me posted on how you do, but don’t let me distract you.

Dyonn: This is good practice for me too try and remember my games. I got the order wrong on she if that, and couldn't remember where 2 of his stones went
C[SteveGKopp: Well played. You made quick work of that corner. Nice invasion.

C[Dyonn: My 2nd game was an intense even game. I won an early fight, but then he managed to loosely enclose a huge area on the other side of the board that I was struggling to break into. I invaded which ended in a ko for life with both of us in byo-yomi. I didn't see any big ko threats, so I took a few points for it, which was enough to win!
Dyonn: no chance for me to rember that game with the next round right after
Dyonn: the last game was another handicap, me giving 5 stones. I had got a lead, but I messed it up and he got back into it enough to win. Too greedy!
C[SteveGKopp: That sounds like a very successful weekend. Well done.

SteveGKopp: I’ve changed my perspective on handicap games. I like Shygost’s perspective that over 4 stones, you should lose. He says the better player should play solid and if the weaker player plays correctly, the match is intended to reinforce them. I’ve been losing more of the handicap games, but at least we are both practicing good fundamentals.

C[Dyonn: interesting. At my old club all games used a handicap system, and our 6dan was up to 10dan on the club ranking. he always got more than he should, and I've probably picked uup the bad habit of trying to do that

C[SteveGKopp: I started out that way. I was told to make wonky and unusual moves to confuse the weaker opponent. Then you just break their shapes and kill their groups. Now I treat anything above 3 as teaching games. It’s nice to let them get the wins also. If a player is DDK, there is no real fun in beating them anyways. I’d say that all the way to 8k.
